sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Delirium: prevention, diagnosis and management in hospital and long-term care
sources.version: NICE clinical guideline CG103, last updated 18 January 2023
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; requires UAE legal and pathway adaptation
sources.population: People aged 18 years and over in hospital or long-term residential or nursing care.
sources.clinical_setting: Hospital and long-term care; the acute-hospital recommendations are partially applicable to emergency assessment.
sources.applicability_note: Exact only for recent fluctuating cognitive, perceptual, physical-function and social-behaviour changes, documentation of those changes, and use of a competent structured assessment. It does not establish delirium or cover every cause of altered mental status.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.1 — risk factors and indicators at presentation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting recent hours-to-days changes or fluctuations in cognition, perception, physical function and social behaviour reported by the person or an informant.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.5.2–1.6.4 — document changes and competent assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting observed changes and, when performed by a competent practitioner, the named structured assessment and clinician-confirmed diagnosis; no diagnosis is inferred by the template.
